ground_truth.title: Best Online MBA Programs
ground_truth.author: Thomas Broderick
ground_truth.main_content: The best online MBA programs prepare you for management-level positions and high-paying roles in business, finance, and even healthcare. Want to improve your career prospects and salary potential in the business world? Then consider applying to online MBA programs. Top MBA programs let you specialize in key business topics like finance, marketing, and management. Full-time students can earn an MBA degree online in two years, while part-time students take around three years. No matter which option you choose, you'll graduate with the skills needed for a successful career in business. We ranked the best online MBA programs using criteria like tuition rates, coursework, support services, and student outcomes. Why You Should Earn Your MBA Degree Online: Some employers require candidates for leadership and executive roles to have an MBA. Asynchronous online MBA classes let you complete coursework on your own time. Out-of-state public universities may offer in-state tuition rates if you enroll in a 100% online program. The best online MBA programs feature the same student services as on-campus programs, including resume support and career services. Online education makes it easier to network with peers living around the U.S. — or even around the world. You can enroll in an out-of-state school without spending money on relocating. Top Online MBA Programs: #1 The University of Texas Rio Grande Valley - Allows you to graduate in as little as 12 months with an accelerated timeline. Cost per credit: $472 in-state and out-of-state. Credits to graduate: 36. AACSB International accredited. #2 University of Minnesota-Twin Cities - Offers strong career outcomes with 62% average salary increase and 39% promotion rate. Flexible completion window of 2-7 years. Cost per credit: $1,797. Credits to graduate: 52. AACSB International accredited. #3 University of Maryland-College Park - Features AI-forward curriculum covering machine learning and modern global disruptions. Lifetime career support available. Cost per credit: $1,733. Credits to graduate: 54. AACSB International accredited. #4 California State University, San Bernardino - Includes a unique bootcamp to build key business skills. Options for accelerated 21-month plan or flexible pace. Cost per credit: $1,100. Credits to graduate: 48. AACSB International accredited. #5 University of Utah - Designed for working professionals with stackable certificates in business analytics, cybersecurity, and information systems. Cost per credit: $1,500. Credits to graduate: 48. AACSB International accredited. Application Requirements for an Online MBA: Completed application and application fee, undergraduate transcripts, resume, statement of purpose, 2-3 letters of recommendation, GMAT or GRE scores if required, interview if required.
with: The best online MBA programs prepare you for management-level positions and high-paying roles in business, finance, and even healthcare.
Full-time students can earn an MBA degree online in two years, while part-time students take around three years.
UTRGV's online MBA allows you to graduate in as little as 12 months. Short, intensive courses let you focus on fewer subjects at once.
UNM graduates in 2024 reported a 62% average salary increase and a 39% promotion/advancement rate within just four months.
The UMD's online MBA stands out for its AI-forward curriculum, covering topics such as machine learning, intelligent technologies, and modern global disruptions.
